subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Help the Shape Studio build fair designs for a classroom mural.
lesson.mini_lesson: Shapes have defining attributes, equal shares must be the same size, and arrays show equal rows and columns.
lesson.objective: Recognize 2D and 3D shapes, describe attributes, partition shapes into equal shares, and understand arrays.
lesson.concept_explanation: A shape name comes from attributes such as sides, corners, or faces. Partitions create equal shares. Arrays organize objects in equal rows and columns.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may use color or size instead of defining attributes, confuse rows with columns, or call unequal parts equal shares.
lesson.real_world_connection: Geometry helps describe boxes, tiles, windows, snacks, and organized groups of objects.
lesson.lesson_summary: Name shapes by attributes, check equal shares, and multiply repeated rows or columns by repeated addition.
hints: Look for defining attributes.
Check that shares are equal sizes.
For arrays, count rows and columns.

worked_examples.text: A triangle has 3 sides and 3 corners no matter which way it is turned.
A rectangle split into 4 equal parts is partitioned into fourths.
An array with 3 rows and 4 columns has 12 objects because 4 + 4 + 4 = 12.

hint_ladder: Check that shares are equal sizes.
For arrays, count rows and columns.

misconception_bank.defining_attribute_confusion: Learner may focus on color, size, or orientation instead of sides, corners, or faces.
misconception_bank.unequal_partition_error: Learner may count parts without checking equal sizes.
misconception_bank.rows_columns_confusion: Learner may swap rows and columns or count only one row.
misconception_bank.shape_label_confusion: Learner may use the wrong 2D or 3D shape name.
